title: Tapping to Celebrate Luxury and Simplicity Together
subtitle: A tapping practice for releasing the idea that wealth and homely comfort are in conflict
excerpt: A five-minute tapping practice from Day 62 of a 12-week money program. The script targets the belief that wealth must look a certain way — that luxury and simplicity cannot coexist. The reframe moves toward abundance that holds both a fine dinner and a bowl of homemade soup without contradiction.
sourceNotes: The tapping framework is Gary Craig's Emotional Freedom Technique (EFT), in use since the mid-1990s. The script is adapted from Day 62 of MONEY: A 12-Week Tapping Program to Attract Cash, Overflow & Abundance Without Hustle or Burnout (Rebecca J Page, 2025, ISBN 9798268793796).

practice.whenToUse: Use when you feel you have to choose between enjoying simple pleasures and building wealth, or when wealth feels like something that would change who you are in ways you don't want.

content.text: A five-minute tapping practice from Day 62 of a 12-week money program. The script targets the internal conflict around what wealth 'should' look like: the worry that affluence means leaving behind the simple pleasures, or that enjoying simple pleasures means you're not serious about wealth. The reframe holds both.
New to tapping? Read
how EFT tapping works
first.
The practice
Karate chop
Even though I once thought wealth had to mean constant luxury, I deeply and completely accept myself.
Even though I worried that simplicity and abundance might clash, I deeply and completely accept myself.
Even though I wasn't sure I could enjoy both, I deeply and completely accept myself.
Tapping round
Eyebrow: I enjoy beautiful things and quiet moments.
Side of eye: I like fine meals and homemade bread.
Under eye: I value both luxury and simplicity.
Under nose: I used to think I had to pick one.
Chin: I thought wealth might drown out the ordinary.
Collarbone: I now see they fit together.
Under arm: I let myself love both sides of life.
Top of head: I celebrate richness in every form.
Reframe to positive
Eyebrow: I welcome abundance that is both grand and gentle.
Side of eye: I savour simple joys and luxurious pleasures equally.
Under eye: I trust wealth to support my actual tastes.
Under nose: I mix elegance with everyday comfort.
Chin: I enjoy a balanced, full life.
Collarbone: I feel rich in every kind of pleasure.
Under arm: I honour both everyday grace and indulgence.
Top of head: I live abundance in all its forms.
Tap each round three times, then move to the reframe and tap that three times.
Where this practice comes from

The tapping framework is Gary Craig's Emotional Freedom Technique (EFT), developed in the mid-1990s from Roger Callahan's Thought Field Therapy. The script is adapted from Day 62 of MONEY: A 12-Week Tapping Program (Rebecca J Page, 2025).
